# US-Iran Negotiations, Strait of Hormuz & Oil Supply — Research Report (as of June 16, 2026)

## Executive Summary

The US and Iran are on the verge of signing a landmark peace deal following the **2026 Iran War** (launched February 28, 2026). On **June 14, 2026**, President Trump announced the deal is "complete," with a signing ceremony scheduled for **June 19 in Geneva, Switzerland**. The Strait of Hormuz — effectively closed since late February — is expected to reopen under the deal terms, though significant implementation risks remain. Oil prices have plunged from a peak of $126.10/bbl to ~$83.63/bbl on deal optimism, but remain elevated above the pre-war ~$72.50 baseline.

---

## 1. War Background & Negotiation Timeline

### The 2026 Iran War
- **February 28, 2026**: The US and Israel launched **"Operation Midnight Hammer"** — coordinated strikes on Iran that **killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei** and destroyed nuclear facilities at Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan. Over 11,000 targets were struck; 3,000+ Iranians killed.
- **Mojtaba Khamenei** (son of the deceased Supreme Leader) was selected as the new Supreme Leader on March 8–9, 2026.
- DNI Tulsi Gabbard testified on March 18 that **Iran's nuclear enrichment capabilities were destroyed**.

### Full Negotiation Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| Feb 6, 2026 | Round 1 of 2026 talks (Muscat, Oman) |
| Feb 15, 2026 | Round 2 (Geneva) |
| Feb 25–26, 2026 | Round 3 — agreement "within reach" (Geneva) |
| **Feb 28, 2026** | **War begins — Operation Midnight Hammer** |
| Apr 7–8, 2026 | Two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan |
| **Apr 11–12, 2026** | **Islamabad Talks** — first direct US-Iran talks since 1979; 21 hours; collapsed without deal |
| Apr 13, 2026 | US imposes naval blockade on Iranian ports |
| May 2, 2026 | Iran submits 14-point ceasefire proposal (rejected by Trump) |
| May 17, 2026 | Trump sets 5 preconditions for deal |
| May 22, 2026 | Qatar joins Pakistan as co-mediator |
| May 24, 2026 | Trump says MOU "close to finalization" |
| **Jun 6, 2026** | Trump announces VP Vance to attend deal signing "in Europe" |
| Jun 10–11, 2026 | IRGC declares "complete closure" of Strait of Hormuz |
| Jun 11, 2026 | Trump announces 60-day ceasefire extension |
| **Jun 14, 2026** | **Trump announces deal is "complete"; authorizes removal of naval blockade** |
| **Jun 19, 2026** | **Signing ceremony scheduled — Geneva, Switzerland** |

### Key Negotiators
- **US**: VP JD Vance, Special Envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper
- **Iran**: FM Abbas Araghchi, Deputy FM Gharibabadi, Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf
- **Mediators**: Pakistan (primary), Qatar (co-mediator from May 22)

### Reported Deal Terms (as of June 14–15)
- Strait of Hormuz to **reopen immediately without tolls**
- **Full restoration of pre-war shipping within ~30 days**
- Ceasefire extension provisions
- **Nuclear and sanctions terms NOT yet publicly disclosed**
- Iran's Deputy FM Gharibabadi confirmed the deal from the Iranian side on June 14

### Trump's Five Preconditions (May 17)
1. Iran must deliver 400 kg of enriched uranium to the US
2. Iran must maintain only one operational nuclear facility
3. Negotiations linked to cessation of hostilities
4. Refusal to release 25%+ of Iran's frozen assets
5. Rejection of Iran's demand for war reparations

---

## 2. Strait of Hormuz Developments

### Closure Status
- The Strait has been **effectively closed to commercial shipping since February 28, 2026**
- S&P Global described it as **"the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy"**
- The Strait normally handles **~25% of world seaborne oil** and **~20% of global LNG**
- **~2,000 ships and ~20,000 mariners from 87 countries** became stranded
- On **June 10–11**, the IRGC declared "complete closure" and published expanded boundary maps

### Key Military Incidents
- **Apr 13**: US imposes naval blockade of Iranian ports; 51+ vessels redirected
- **May 4**: Trump announces "Project Freedom" — US naval escort operation (3 Carrier Strike Groups, 2 Marine Expeditionary Units)
- **May 4–5**: Iran attacks UAE Fujairah oil facilities (12 ballistic missiles, 3 cruise missiles, 4 drones)
- **May 7**: US strikes Iranian military at Bandar Abbas and Qeshm Island

### Major Risks to Reopening
1. **IRGC has NOT publicly endorsed the deal** — critical given their operational control
2. An Iranian lawmaker stated the Strait "will never return to the status quo that existed before the war"
3. **Fitch** assessed broad Hormuz reopening unlikely before July
4. Insurance companies remain largely unwilling to cover Hormuz transit
5. Metaculus community forecast: **40% probability of reopening before June 27**

---

## 3. Oil Supply & Price Impact

### Iran's Oil Export Situation
- Iran's oil exports have been **effectively zero** since the US naval blockade on April 13, 2026
- Iran is losing an estimated **$435 million per day** from the blockade
- Iran's total economic damage: estimated **$300 billion to $1 trillion**

### Global Oil Supply Impact
- **Global oil supply loss**: ~2 million barrels per day (IEA estimate)
- **US Strategic Petroleum Reserve release**: 17.5 million barrels (March–April)
- **OPEC+ response**: Modest 188,000 bpd increase starting June — far below the ~2M bpd loss
- **UAE departed OPEC** during the crisis
- **Venezuela** stepped in: exports reached 1.23 million bpd (7-year high); US imports from Venezuela hit 588,000 bpd

### Brent Crude Price Trajectory

| Date | Price ($/bbl) | Context |
|------|--------------|---------|
| Feb 27 (pre-war) | ~$72.50 | Baseline |
| Late April (peak) | $126.10 | 52-week high |
| Jun 8 | $94.25 | Declining on deal hopes |
| **Jun 15** | **$83.63** | -25.4% from May high |

- Annualized volatility: **41.5%** — exceptionally elevated
- Futures curve in **backwardation** (Aug $83.63 → Nov $80.64)

### Broader Economic Impact
- US gasoline peak: **$4.45/gal** (+49% from pre-war $2.98)
- US war cost: ~$25–29 billion (Apr–May)
- Q1 2026 US PCE inflation: **+4.5% annualized**
- Euro area energy inflation: **10.9%** (April 2026)
- Goldman Sachs recession probability: **30%** (12-month)

---

## 4. Prediction Markets (as of June 15)

| Market | Question | Probability |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| Metaculus #44056 | US & Iran sign agreement before June 26? | **75%** |
| Metaculus #44050 | Iran reopen Strait of Hormuz before June 27? | **40%** |
| Polymarket | US and Iran sign agreement (by Jul 31) | **~81.5%** |

---

## 5. Data Limitations

Research data was last updated **June 15, 2026**. Live web search tools were unavailable for this session, so developments on June 16 could not be verified. The most critical unknown is whether any changes have occurred affecting the scheduled **June 19 signing ceremony**.

---

key_facts: On February 28, 2026, the US and Israel launched 'Operation Midnight Hammer' against Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and destroying nuclear facilities at Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan.
The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed to commercial shipping since February 28, 2026 — described by S&P Global as 'the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy.'
On June 14, 2026, Trump announced the US-Iran deal is 'complete,' with a signing ceremony scheduled for June 19, 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland.
Iran's Deputy FM Gharibabadi confirmed the deal from the Iranian side on June 14, 2026.
Deal terms include immediate reopening of the Strait of Hormuz without tolls and full restoration of pre-war shipping within ~30 days; nuclear and sanctions terms remain undisclosed.
The IRGC has NOT publicly endorsed the deal — a critical risk given their operational control of the Strait closure.
Iran's oil exports have been effectively zero since the US naval blockade imposed April 13, 2026.
Global oil supply loss estimated at ~2 million barrels per day (IEA).
Brent crude surged from ~$72.50/bbl pre-war to a peak of $126.10/bbl in late April, then fell to $83.63/bbl by June 15 on deal optimism.
US gasoline prices peaked at $4.45/gal (+49% from pre-war $2.98/gal).
Key mediators are Pakistan (primary) and Qatar (co-mediator from May 22); key US negotiators include VP Vance, Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner.
Prediction markets as of June 15: Metaculus gives 75% probability of a signed agreement before June 26; Polymarket gives ~81.5% by July 31.
Metaculus gives only 40% probability of Strait of Hormuz reopening before June 27, reflecting implementation risks.
Fitch assessed broad Hormuz reopening as unlikely before July 2026.
Venezuela stepped in as an offset supplier, reaching 1.23 million bpd exports (7-year high); US imports from Venezuela hit 588,000 bpd.
OPEC+ response was modest: only 188,000 bpd increase starting June — far below the ~2M bpd supply loss.
UAE departed OPEC during the crisis.
Q1 2026 US PCE inflation rose to +4.5% annualized; Goldman Sachs put 12-month US recession probability at 30%.
